index.seoTitle: Free strength programs: GZCLP, 5x5 and more | Gravity Room
index.seoDescription: Compare strength and hypertrophy programs and inspect the full schedule before starting. Free tracker with automatic progression.
index.heading: Workout programs
index.intro: Explore strength and hypertrophy programs by experience level. Every page shows the full schedule and program rules, so you can make an informed choice before logging your first workout.
index.chooseBody: Both are linear progression programs for beginners, but they organize volume, frequency and failure response differently.

comparison.seoTitle: GZCLP vs StrongLifts 5x5: differences and choice | Gravity Room
comparison.seoDescription: A direct GZCLP and StrongLifts 5x5 comparison covering frequency, structure, progression and failure rules for beginners.
comparison.heading: GZCLP vs StrongLifts 5x5
comparison.answer: StrongLifts 5x5 prioritizes simplicity: three weekly sessions, two alternating workouts and five sets of five for the main lifts. GZCLP uses T1, T2 and T3 tiers, a four-workout rotation and rep-scheme changes after failure. Choose StrongLifts for fewer decisions; choose GZCLP for a more detailed balance of strength and volume.

sections.heading: Frequency and structure
sections.body: StrongLifts alternates two A/B workouts on three non-consecutive days each week and includes squats in every session. GZCLP rotates four workouts, each combining a T1 main lift, T2 secondary work and T3 accessories.

sections.heading: How load progresses
sections.body: Both programs add load while you complete the prescribed work. In Gravity Room's catalog, StrongLifts adds 2.5 kg per session and 5 kg for deadlifts. GZCLP applies linear progression across its tiers and changes sets and reps after failure.

sections.heading: What happens after failure
sections.body: StrongLifts applies a 10% deload after three consecutive failures on a lift. GZCLP moves T1 from 5x3 to 6x2 and later resets after the next stage. Each program page exposes these rules before you start.

sections.heading: Which one should you choose?
sections.body: StrongLifts fits lifters who value a highly repeatable weekly pattern. GZCLP offers more rep variety and a clear split between main, secondary and accessory work. The better choice is the one compatible with your schedule and equipment.

comparison.ctaBody: Open the interactive program pages to see every workout and start tracking your chosen program for free.

progression.seoTitle: Automatic strength progression: how it works | Gravity Room
progression.seoDescription: A practical progressive overload guide: when to add weight, what to do after failure and how a tracker applies each program's rules.
progression.heading: Automatic progression and progressive overload
progression.answer: Progressive overload means gradually increasing a training variable such as load or repetitions. Within a defined program, automatic progression applies its rules after you log the result: it raises the load after success or activates the appropriate failure and deload stage.

sections.heading: Complete the prescription first
sections.body: You do not need to add weight to every set. Log the sets, repetitions and outcome prescribed by the program. The next workout is calculated from that outcome and the movement's specific rules.

sections.heading: Linear progression
sections.body: In linear progression, load increases by a defined amount after a completed session. StrongLifts and GZCLP use this principle, although their frequency, increments, volume and failure protocols differ.

sections.heading: Double progression
sections.body: For accessory work, some programs increase repetitions within a range before adding weight. This avoids forcing a load jump when available equipment does not offer small increments.

sections.heading: Failures and deloads
sections.body: Missing a prescription does not require improvisation. A program may hold the load, change sets and reps or reduce weight. Gravity Room stores that rule in the program definition and applies it when you log the result.

sections.heading: What the tracker automates
sections.body: The tracker calculates the next workout from the selected definition, displays the schedule and retains history. Automation executes program rules; it does not replace choosing a reasonable starting load or using sound technique.

progression.ctaBody: Inspect GZCLP and StrongLifts 5x5 to compare two concrete applications of linear progression.

calendar_navigator.history_microcopy: Real dates appear when you complete workouts.

program_preview.error_details: The requested program does not exist or could not be loaded.
program_preview.cta_signup_prompt: Create an account to track your progress
program_preview.cta_active_warning: Finish your current program before starting a new one.

active_card.orphan_description: This program's definition no longer exists. Delete this instance to start a new one.
active_card.orphan_confirm_message: All associated results will be deleted. Continue?
active_card.recent_activity_empty: Complete your first workout to see recent activity.

gzclp.name: GZCLP
gzclp.description: How to train on Kaio-sama's planet: linear progression under constant pressure. 4-day rotation with T1, T2, and T3 exercises. Fail a lift and you switch stages and keep fighting. Inspired by Cody LeFever's GZCL method.
gzclp.seoTitle: GZCLP Linear Progression, 4-Day Program | Gravity Room
gzclp.seoDescription: GZCLP is a beginner program based on Cody LeFever's GZCL method: 4-day rotation with T1, T2, T3 tiers and linear progression (5x3, 6x2, deload).

faq.question: Is GZCLP good for beginners?
faq.answer: Yes. GZCLP is a beginner-level program built on the GZCL method, using linear progression so you add weight steadily over a 4-day training rotation.

faq.question: How many days a week is GZCLP?
faq.answer: GZCLP runs on a 4-day rotation, with each session built around T1, T2 and T3 tier exercises.

faq.question: What happens in GZCLP when you fail a lift?
faq.answer: When you fail, you change stage rather than stalling: the progression moves from 5x3 to 6x2 and then to a deload before resetting.

hexan-ppl.name: HeXaN PPL
hexan-ppl.description: Six days of Kaioken: push your body to the limit with strength and volume. Push/Pull/Legs combining 5/3/1 on the main compounds with double progression on accessories. Inspired by HeXaN's methodology.
hexan-ppl.seoTitle: 5/3/1 Push Pull Legs, 6 Days/Week - HeXaN | Gravity Room
hexan-ppl.seoDescription: 5/3/1 Push/Pull/Legs program, 6 days a week: 5/3/1 on the main compound lifts plus double progression on accessories. Free workout tracker.

faq.question: How many days a week is the HeXaN PPL program?
faq.answer: HeXaN PPL runs 6 days a week, training Push, Pull and Legs twice each week.

faq.question: How does progression work in HeXaN PPL?
faq.answer: Main compound lifts progress via 5/3/1, while accessory exercises use double progression: add reps first, then weight.

faq.question: What training method does HeXaN PPL use?
faq.answer: It combines a Push/Pull/Legs split with 5/3/1 for the main compound lifts and double progression on accessories, inspired by the HeXaN methodology.

stronglifts-5x5.name: StrongLifts 5x5
stronglifts-5x5.description: A classic beginner strength program. Two alternating workouts (A/B), 3 days per week. Squat every session, linear progression of +2.5 kg per workout (+5 kg on deadlift). Three consecutive failures trigger a 10% deload.
stronglifts-5x5.seoDescription: StrongLifts 5x5 by Mehdi Hadim: a beginner strength program, 3 days/week, alternating A/B workouts with squats every session and linear +2.5 kg progression.

faq.question: How many days a week is StrongLifts 5x5?
faq.answer: StrongLifts 5x5 runs 3 days per week, alternating two workouts (A and B) on non-consecutive days.

faq.question: Is StrongLifts 5x5 good for beginners?
faq.answer: Yes, it is a classic beginner strength program built around simple linear progression and the main barbell lifts.

faq.question: How does weight progression work in StrongLifts 5x5?
faq.answer: You add +2.5 kg each workout (+5 kg on deadlift); after three consecutive failures on a lift you deload that lift by 10%.

phraks-greyskull-lp.name: Phrak's Greyskull LP
phraks-greyskull-lp.description: Phrakture's beginner strength program. Two alternating workouts (A/B), 3 days per week. Every exercise ends with an AMRAP set (to technical failure). Linear progression with a 10% deload on failure.
phraks-greyskull-lp.seoTitle: Phrak's Greyskull LP: 3-Day Beginner | Gravity Room
phraks-greyskull-lp.seoDescription: Phrak's Greyskull LP: beginner strength program, 3 days/week, alternating A/B workouts with an AMRAP final set and linear progression (10% deload on failure).

faq.question: Is Phrak's Greyskull LP good for beginners?
faq.answer: Yes. It is a beginner-level linear-progression strength program with a simple A/B structure designed to add weight each session.

faq.question: How many days a week is Phrak's Greyskull LP?
faq.answer: It runs 3 days per week, alternating between two workouts (A and B).

faq.question: What happens when you fail a lift on Phrak's Greyskull LP?
faq.answer: When you fail to progress, the weight is reduced by 10% (a deload) and you climb back up via linear progression. Each exercise also ends with an AMRAP final set.

531-boring-but-big.name: 5/3/1 Boring But Big
531-boring-but-big.description: Master Roshi's secret to strength: boring reps, huge results. 4-week cycles (5s, 3s, 5/3/1, deload) with 5×10 supplemental volume. 4 days per week. Inspired by Jim Wendler's 5/3/1 BBB.
531-boring-but-big.seoTitle: 5/3/1 Boring But Big — 4-Day Strength | Gravity Room
531-boring-but-big.seoDescription: Wendler 5/3/1 Boring But Big tracker: 4-day intermediate strength program, 4-week cycles (5s, 3s, 5/3/1, deload) with 5x10 supplemental volume.

faq.question: How many days a week is 5/3/1 Boring But Big?
faq.answer: 5/3/1 Boring But Big runs 4 days per week, organized in 4-week cycles.

faq.question: What is the structure of 5/3/1 BBB?
faq.answer: Each 4-week cycle follows a 5s week, a 3s week, a 5/3/1 week, and a deload week, with 5x10 supplemental volume on top of the main lifts.

faq.question: Is 5/3/1 Boring But Big good for intermediate lifters?
faq.answer: Yes. It is classified as an intermediate strength program, built on Jim Wendler's 5/3/1 BBB and designed to add muscle and strength through high-volume 5x10 supplemental work.

531-for-beginners.name: 5/3/1 for Beginners
531-for-beginners.description: Full-body training at Kami's Lookout: 3 days per week, two main lifts per session. 3-week cycles (5s, 3s, 5/3/1) with FSL 5×5 as supplemental work. Methodical and balanced. Inspired by Jim Wendler's 5/3/1 for Beginners.
531-for-beginners.seoDescription: Jim Wendler's 5/3/1 for Beginners: full-body strength, 3 days/week, two main lifts per session, 3-week 5s/3s/5-3-1 cycles with FSL 5x5 supplemental.

faq.question: How many days a week is 5/3/1 for Beginners?
faq.answer: It runs 3 days per week, training the full body each session with two main lifts per workout.

faq.question: Is 5/3/1 for Beginners good for new lifters?
faq.answer: Yes. It's Jim Wendler's beginner-oriented 5/3/1 variant, using simple full-body sessions and a structured 3-week cycle to build strength.

faq.question: What is the structure and progression of 5/3/1 for Beginners?
faq.answer: It uses 3-week cycles (5s, 3s, then 5/3/1) on the main lifts, with First Set Last (FSL) 5x5 as the supplemental work.

phul.name: PHUL
phul.description: Strength and size like Vegeta in his gravity chamber: two days of pure power (heavy compounds, 3–5 reps) and two of hypertrophy (8–12 reps). 4 days per week. Inspired by Brandon Campbell's PHUL.
phul.seoTitle: PHUL Workout: 4-Day Power & Hypertrophy | Gravity Room
phul.seoDescription: PHUL (Power Hypertrophy Upper Lower) is a 4-day intermediate split: 2 power days (heavy compounds, 3-5 reps) and 2 hypertrophy days (8-12 reps).

faq.question: How many days a week is the PHUL program?
faq.answer: PHUL runs 4 days per week: two power days and two hypertrophy days, each split into upper and lower body sessions.

faq.question: What is the difference between power and hypertrophy days in PHUL?
faq.answer: Power days use heavy compound lifts in the 3-5 rep range to build strength, while hypertrophy days use 8-12 reps to drive muscle growth.

faq.question: Is PHUL good for intermediate lifters?
faq.answer: Yes. PHUL is an intermediate-level program, based on Brandon Campbell's Power Hypertrophy Upper Lower split, that combines strength and size work across 4 weekly sessions.

nivel-7.name: Level 7
nivel-7.description: A 12-week strength program with reverse periodization. You set your target record (week 6) and weights are calculated backwards. Block 1 (5×5) with a deload in week 3, Block 2 (3×3) peaking at the record. Cycle 2 repeats the wave with +2.5 kg. Accessories use double progression 3×8–12. 4 days/week: shoulders/triceps, back/calves, chest/biceps, legs.
nivel-7.seoTitle: 12-Week Reverse Periodization Strength, 4 Days | Gravity Room
nivel-7.seoDescription: 12-week reverse-periodization strength program, 4 days/week: Block 1 5x5, Block 2 3x3, 3x8-12 accessories. Set a week-6 target record; weights back-calculate.

faq.question: How many days per week is this 12-week strength program?
faq.answer: It runs 4 days per week across 12 weeks, structured as Block 1 (5x5) followed by Block 2 (3x3).

faq.question: How does reverse periodization work in this program?
faq.answer: You set a target record for week 6 and the program calculates the weights backward from it, so earlier weeks build up to that planned peak.

faq.question: What set and rep scheme does this program use?
faq.answer: The main work is Block 1 at 5x5 and Block 2 at 3x3, with accessories run as 3x8-12 using double progression.

caparazon-de-tortuga.name: Turtle Shell
caparazon-de-tortuga.description: Your training at Kame House: start from zero with bodyweight and gradually add the bar. 200 sessions, 3 days/week. 4 blocks per session: Core, Activation, Proprioception, and the Fundamental Lift. Inspired by Amerigo Brunetti's methodology.
caparazon-de-tortuga.seoDescription: Beginner strength program based on Amerigo Brunetti's bodyweight-to-barbell method: 200 sessions, 3 days/week, 4 blocks per session.

faq.question: Is the Amerigo Brunetti bodyweight-to-barbell method good for beginners?
faq.answer: Yes. It is a beginner-level strength program that starts from zero with bodyweight exercises and gradually adds the barbell, so no prior lifting experience is needed.

faq.question: How many days a week and how many sessions is this program?
faq.answer: It runs 3 days per week across 200 total sessions, making it a long-term progression from bodyweight toward barbell training.

faq.question: How is each session structured?
faq.answer: Every session has 4 blocks: Core, Activation, Proprioception, and the Fundamental Exercise (main lift), following Amerigo Brunetti's methodology.

365-programmare-lipertrofia.name: 365 Programmare l'Ipertrofia
365-programmare-lipertrofia.description: Amerigo Brunetti's year-long hypertrophy program, structured in 5 phases across 212 sessions. Phase Zero (8 weeks): technique with minimal loads. Phase T1 (6 weeks): introducing load on the three fundamental lifts. Phase PN (13 weeks): progressive ramping with specific overloads. Phase JAW (18 weeks): 3 independent intensification blocks with their own TMs and a 1RM test at the end of each. Phase IS (12 weeks): isolation and consolidation work, 12–30 reps. 4 days per week.

la-sala-del-tiempo.name: Hyperbolic Time Chamber
la-sala-del-tiempo.description: Inspired by Amerigo Brunetti's methodology. Your year in the Hyperbolic Time Chamber: 196 sessions of hypertrophy structured in 4 phases of increasing intensity (T1, PN, JAW, IS). Only for warriors who already own the squat, bench press, and deadlift. 4 days per week.

sala-del-tiempo-1.name: Hyperbolic Time Chamber 1
sala-del-tiempo-1.description: Phase T1 — Perfezionamento Tecnico: 24 sessions of technique with light loads (40–70% TM). Isometrics, tempos, and movement control on the squat, bench press, and deadlift. 6 weeks, 4 days/week. Inspired by Amerigo Brunetti's methodology.
sala-del-tiempo-1.seoTitle: Brunetti 365 Phase T1 Technique - 4 days/wk | Gravity Room
sala-del-tiempo-1.seoDescription: Brunetti 365 phase T1 technique block: 24 sessions, 6 weeks, 4 days/week of tempo, isometric and control work at 40-70% TM on squat, bench, deadlift.

faq.question: What is the Brunetti 365 T1 technique phase?
faq.answer: It is the T1 phase (T1 — Perfezionamento Tecnico) of Amerigo Brunetti's 365 hypertrophy program: 24 sessions of technique work with light loads at 40-70% of your training max, focused on tempo, isometrics and control on the squat, bench press and deadlift.

faq.question: How many days a week and weeks long is the program?
faq.answer: It runs 4 days per week for 6 weeks, totaling 24 sessions.

faq.question: What does this T1 technique phase focus on?
faq.answer: It focuses on refining squat, bench press and deadlift technique through tempo work, isometrics and control with light loads (40-70% training max).

sala-del-tiempo-2.name: Hyperbolic Time Chamber 2
sala-del-tiempo-2.description: Phase PN — Potenziamento Neurale: 52 sessions of progressive neural potentiation (60–95% TM). Load ramping and strength sets to carry technique over to heavier weights. 13 weeks, 4 days/week. Inspired by Amerigo Brunetti's methodology.
sala-del-tiempo-2.seoTitle: Brunetti 365 PN Phase, 4-Day Hypertrophy | Gravity Room
sala-del-tiempo-2.seoDescription: Brunetti 365 PN neural potentiation phase: 13-week, 4-day/week hypertrophy block, 52 sessions with load ramping and strength sets at 60-95% of training max.

faq.question: What is the Brunetti 365 PN phase?
faq.answer: It is the Potenziamento Neurale (neural potentiation) phase of Amerigo Brunetti's 365 hypertrophy program: 52 sessions of progressive neural potentiation working between 60-95% of your training max with load ramping and strength sets.

faq.question: How many days a week and how long is the PN phase?
faq.answer: The PN phase runs 13 weeks at 4 days per week, totaling 52 training sessions.

faq.question: What does the Brunetti 365 PN phase involve?
faq.answer: It is a hypertrophy block built on progressive neural potentiation, with load ramping and strength sets that work between 60-95% of your training max across 52 sessions.

sala-del-tiempo-3.name: Hyperbolic Time Chamber 3
sala-del-tiempo-3.description: Phase JAW Mod — 72 sessions of intensification across 3 six-week blocks (60 → 70 → 80% TM). Each block ends with a 1RM test that feeds the next block's TM. 18 weeks, 4 days/week. Inspired by Amerigo Brunetti's methodology.
sala-del-tiempo-3.seoTitle: Brunetti 365 JAW Phase, 4-Day Intermediate | Gravity Room
sala-del-tiempo-3.seoDescription: Brunetti 365 JAW phase: 18-week intermediate hypertrophy program, 4 days/week, 72 sessions in three 6-week blocks (60-70-80% TM), each ending in a 1RM test.

faq.question: What is the Brunetti 365 JAW phase?
faq.answer: It is the JAW (intensification) phase of Amerigo Brunetti's 365 hypertrophy program: 72 sessions across three 6-week blocks at 60%, 70% and 80% of training max, with a 1RM test at the end of each block.

faq.question: How many days a week is the Brunetti 365 JAW program?
faq.answer: It runs 4 days per week over 18 weeks, totaling 72 intensification sessions.

faq.question: Who is the Brunetti 365 JAW phase for?
faq.answer: It targets intermediate lifters focused on hypertrophy who want progressive intensification, since each 6-week block steps up the load from 60% to 70% to 80% of training max.

tenkaichi-budokai-sentadilla.name: Tenkaichi Budokai — Squat
tenkaichi-budokai-sentadilla.description: A 16-week tournament prep with a squat focus. Exact percentages of your 1RM, mesocycle periodization, deload before competition. 4 days per week. Inspired by Boris Sheiko's methodology.
tenkaichi-budokai-sentadilla.seoTitle: Sheiko Squat Program, 16 Weeks 4 Days | Gravity Room
tenkaichi-budokai-sentadilla.seoDescription: Boris Sheiko-inspired 16-week squat-focused powerlifting prep: 4 days/week, exact 1RM percentages, mesocycle periodization and a pre-meet deload.

faq.question: How many days a week is this Sheiko squat program?
faq.answer: It runs 4 days per week across a 16-week block with a squat emphasis.

faq.question: Who is this Boris Sheiko-inspired program for?
faq.answer: It is a squat-focused powerlifting prep for lifters peaking for a meet, using exact 1RM percentages and mesocycle periodization.

faq.question: Does this program include a deload before competition?
faq.answer: Yes. It periodizes training across mesocycles and schedules a deload before competing to peak for a meet.

tenkaichi-budokai-press-banca.name: Tenkaichi Budokai — Bench Press
tenkaichi-budokai-press-banca.description: A 16-week tournament prep with a bench focus. Exact percentages of your 1RM, multiple bench variations. 4 days per week. Inspired by Boris Sheiko's methodology.
tenkaichi-budokai-press-banca.seoTitle: Sheiko Bench Program, 16 Weeks 4 Days | Gravity Room
tenkaichi-budokai-press-banca.seoDescription: Sheiko-inspired 16-week powerlifting bench specialization, 4 days/week, percentage-based off your 1RM with multiple bench variations. For advanced lifters.

faq.question: How long is this Sheiko-inspired bench program?
faq.answer: It runs 16 weeks of bench-emphasis powerlifting prep, training 4 days per week.

faq.question: Is this Sheiko bench program suitable for beginners?
faq.answer: No. It is an advanced program that uses exact percentages of your 1RM and multiple bench variations, so it assumes an established 1RM and lifting experience.

faq.question: How is the load programmed in this bench program?
faq.answer: Every session is percentage-based off your current 1RM, with several bench press variations spread across 4 training days a week.

tenkaichi-budokai-peso-muerto.name: Tenkaichi Budokai — Deadlift
tenkaichi-budokai-peso-muerto.description: A 16-week tournament prep with a deadlift focus. Deficit, blocks, chains, bands — every deadlift variation. 4 days per week. Inspired by Boris Sheiko's methodology.
tenkaichi-budokai-peso-muerto.seoTitle: Sheiko Deadlift Prep, 4 Days/Week | Gravity Room
tenkaichi-budokai-peso-muerto.seoDescription: Boris Sheiko-inspired 16-week deadlift-focused powerlifting prep: deficit pulls, training blocks, chains and bands, 4 days per week.

faq.question: How many days a week is this Sheiko-style deadlift program?
faq.answer: It runs 4 days per week across a 16-week powerlifting preparation cycle with deadlift emphasis.

faq.question: What is this Boris Sheiko-inspired program focused on?
faq.answer: It is a 16-week powerlifting preparation block centered on building the deadlift.

faq.question: What deadlift variations and tools does it use?
faq.answer: It emphasizes the deadlift using deficit pulls, training blocks, and accommodating resistance from chains and bands.

tenkaichi-budokai-solo-banca.name: Tenkaichi Budokai — Bench Only
tenkaichi-budokai-solo-banca.description: An 18-week prep focused entirely on the bench press. No competition squat or deadlift — all energy going into a single strike. 4 days per week. Inspired by Boris Sheiko's methodology.
tenkaichi-budokai-solo-banca.seoTitle: Sheiko Bench-Only Program, 4 Days/Week | Gravity Room
tenkaichi-budokai-solo-banca.seoDescription: An 18-week, bench-press-only powerlifting program inspired by Boris Sheiko, 4 days/week, with no competition squat or deadlift.

faq.question: How many weeks and days per week is this Sheiko bench-only program?
faq.answer: It runs for 18 weeks at 4 training days per week, focused exclusively on the bench press.

faq.question: Does this program include squat and deadlift?
faq.answer: No. It is a bench-press-only specialization with no competition squat or deadlift; all sessions center on the bench press.

faq.question: Who is this Boris Sheiko bench program for?
faq.answer: It is a powerlifting program for lifters who want to peak their bench press, inspired by Boris Sheiko's bench-only methodology.

tenkaichi-budokai-veterano.name: Tenkaichi Budokai — Veteran
tenkaichi-budokai-veterano.description: Sheiko's most popular program for experienced warriors. 4 prep mesocycles with moderate volume and a built-in 1RM test. Use your combat experience to reach the tournament at your best. 4 days per week. Inspired by Boris Sheiko's methodology.
tenkaichi-budokai-veterano.seoTitle: Sheiko Advanced Program, 4 Days/Week | Gravity Room
tenkaichi-budokai-veterano.seoDescription: Boris Sheiko's most popular powerlifting program for experienced lifters: 4 mesocycles, medium volume, built-in 1RM test, 4 days/week. Free tracker.

faq.question: Who is this Sheiko-inspired program for?
faq.answer: It is designed for advanced, experienced powerlifters. It is Boris Sheiko's most popular program, built around 4 medium-volume preparation mesocycles with a built-in 1RM test.

faq.question: How many days a week is the Sheiko program?
faq.answer: It runs 4 days per week, organized into 4 preparation mesocycles with a built-in 1RM test.

faq.question: Does this program test your 1-rep max?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes a 1RM test integrated into the program, so you can measure strength gains after the 4 preparation mesocycles.

furia-oscura.name: Dark Fury
furia-oscura.description: Train like a Saiyan: Push/Pull/Legs with A/B weekly alternation. 3 days/week, Hyperbolic Time Chamber style, double progression until you break your limits.
furia-oscura.seoTitle: Push Pull Legs A/B, 3 Days/Week | Gravity Room
furia-oscura.seoDescription: Push/Pull/Legs A/B split, 3 days a week with weekly A/B alternation and double progression. Track this routine free on Gravity Room.

faq.question: How many days a week is this Push/Pull/Legs program?
faq.answer: It runs 3 days a week, alternating between A and B sessions each week so you cover Push, Pull, and Legs across the rotation.

faq.question: How does the A/B alternation work?
faq.answer: You train 3 days a week and swap between the A and B versions of the Push, Pull, and Legs sessions from one week to the next, so the rotation stays varied.

faq.question: How does progression work on this program?
faq.answer: It uses double progression: you add reps within a target range and then increase the weight once you hit the top of the range.

setup_form.edit_description_default: Update your starting weights — the program will recalculate with new values
setup_form.confirm_message: Your success/fail history will be preserved, but projected weights will change. Continue?

toolbar.finish_confirm_message: Your progress and statistics will be saved. You can review them anytime from the dashboard.
toolbar.reset_confirm_message: Are you sure you want to reset ALL progress? This cannot be undone.

stats_panel.empty_description: Complete your first workout to see statistics and charts.

graduation.completed_description: You have achieved all objectives. You are ready for the JAW Protocol.

page.title: Insights

privacy.meta_description: Gravity Room privacy policy: what data we store, optional cloud sync, and your rights. Free, open-source weightlifting tracker.
privacy.title: Privacy Policy

data_stored.title: Data We Store
data_stored.body: All training data (exercises, weights, sets, repetitions, and results) is stored locally in your browser's localStorage. This data never leaves your device unless you explicitly enable cloud sync.

cloud_sync.title: Cloud Sync (Optional)
cloud_sync.body: If you create an account, we store your email address and training data in a secure database (PostgreSQL). This allows synchronization between devices. Cloud sync is completely optional — the app works fully offline without an account.

google_login.title: Google Sign-In (Optional)
google_login.body: If you sign in with Google, we receive your email address and profile name from Google. We do not access your contacts, calendar, or any other Google data.

cookies.title: Cookies, Analytics, and Tracking
cookies.body_prefix: We only use a technical cookie necessary to maintain authenticated user sessions. We do not use analytics, tracking, or advertising cookies. No data is shared with advertisers or third parties. See our

delete_data.title: Delete Your Data
delete_data.local_body: Clear your browser's localStorage or use the "Reset All" option in the app.
delete_data.cloud_body: If you have an account and wish to delete your cloud data, contact us and we will delete your account and all associated data from our database.

contact.title: Contact
contact.body: For privacy questions or data deletion requests, open an issue on the project repository or contact the maintainer.

cookie_policy.meta_description: Gravity Room cookie policy: which cookies we use and why. Privacy-friendly analytics, no advertising or tracking cookies.
cookie_policy.title: Cookie Policy

what_are_cookies.title: What are cookies?
what_are_cookies.body: Cookies are small text files that websites store in your browser. They may be necessary for the technical functioning of the site or used for analytical or advertising purposes.

which_cookies.title: What cookies does Gravity Room use?
which_cookies.body: Gravity Room only uses a technical cookie strictly necessary for authentication functionality.
which_cookies.no_tracking: We do not use analytics, advertising, or tracking cookies of any kind.

table.name: Name
table.refresh_token_purpose: Maintain authenticated user session. Contains an opaque identifier (no personal data). It is httpOnly and not accessible by JavaScript.

third_party.title: Third-party cookies
third_party.body_prefix: When using Google sign-in, Google's service may set its own cookies during the authentication process. These cookies are managed by Google and subject to their
third_party.no_access: Gravity Room does not control or have access to such cookies.

local_storage.title: Local storage
local_storage.body: In addition to the cookie mentioned above, Gravity Room uses browser local storage to save display preferences and training data in guest mode. This data is never sent to any server and can be deleted by clearing your site data in your browser.

analytics.title: Analytics
analytics.body: a privacy-friendly web analytics service. Plausible does not use cookies, does not collect identifiable personal data, and does not track users between sessions. Aggregated data (page views, country of origin, device type) is used solely to improve the service.

legal_basis.title: Legal basis
legal_basis.body: is exempt from prior consent under article 22.2 of Law 34/2002 (LSSI-CE), as it is strictly necessary for the provision of the authentication service expressly requested by the user. This exemption is also recognized by Recital 66 of Directive 2009/136/EC and the guidelines of the European Data Protection Board.

disable_cookies.title: How to disable cookies?
disable_cookies.body_prefix: You can configure your browser to block or delete cookies. Note that if you block the cookie
disable_cookies.body_suffix: you will not be able to maintain your session and will need to authenticate again on each visit. The app will continue to work in guest mode without cookies.

contact.title: Contact
contact.body: For questions about cookie usage, open an issue on the project repository or contact the maintainer.

delete_account.title: Delete Account
delete_account.description: This action will mark your account for deletion. Your data will be permanently deleted after 30 days.

cookie_banner.message: This site uses a technical cookie necessary to maintain your session. We do not use tracking cookies. We use Plausible Analytics (no cookies) to improve the service.

error_boundary.title: Something went wrong
error_boundary.description: An unexpected error occurred. Reloading the page should fix it.

toast.provider_error: useToast must be used within ToastProvider

guest.provider_error: useGuest must be used within GuestProvider

empty.guest_body: Pick a program from the catalog and start logging your training instantly. Create an account whenever you want to save your progress.
empty.no_program_body: Pick a program to start logging your training.

guest_resume.body: Pick up your training right where you left off.

pristine.title: Your first session is waiting
pristine.body: Open the tracker and log your first workout. Your streak and sessions start counting from there.

mentor_tips: If you fail, you drop a stage, not the bar. Get back under it.
Progress is not linear. Consistency is.
The weight will not lift itself.
Every workout is a debt your body will repay.
Controlled weight beats ego on the floor.
home.load_error: Could not load your training dashboard.

prompt.title: Welcome! Would you like a quick tutorial?

home.label: Home
home.hint: Your active program lives here. Check your streak, weekly sessions, and consistency at a glance.

programs.label: Programs
programs.hint: Browse the catalog, preview routines, and start a new program from here.

preview.label: Program Preview
preview.hint: Explore any program's full schedule before committing. Tap a workout to see the exercises.

tracker.label: Tracker
tracker.hint: Log success, fail, AMRAP, and RPE for every set. The calendar shows your progress over time.

profile.label: Profile
profile.hint: View your stats, badges, volume charts, and program history here.

program_overview.stages_description: {{count}} stages are available if you miss the prescribed sets. Each stage adjusts the sets and reps so you can keep progressing.

route_error.title: Something went wrong
route_error.description: An unexpected error occurred. Reloading the page should fix it.

offline_banner.message: Offline. Changes will be saved when you are back online

chart.empty: Complete workouts to see the chart

rules.advance_stage: Move to the next stage (fewer reps, more sets)
rules.add_weight_reset_stage: Add {{amount}} kg and reset to the first stage
rules.double_progression: Add reps ({{bottom}}-{{top}}); at the top, add weight

triggers.mid_program_fail: Fail in the middle of the program

exerciseCatalog.heading: Exercise catalog
exerciseCatalog.subtitle: Search and filter across the 800+ exercises in the catalog.

empty.title: No results
empty.hint: Try a different search or adjust the filters.
